location.heading: How Video Training Sustains Change at Scale

findings.sentence: Create engaging training videos in 160+ languages.

A comprehensive review of organizational change research describes a steady increase in the pace and complexity of change across sectors ( [Cummings & Worley, 2025](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/10.1080/23311975.2025.2478447)).

Research reviewing determinants of change success finds many initiatives struggle during implementation and follow-through, even when planning is strong ( [Al-Haddad & Kotnour, 2015](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/351455727_The_determinants_of_organizational_change_management_success_Literature_review_and_case_study)).

A systematic review of established change models identified numerous recurring success factors, with leadership alignment, communication quality, and stakeholder engagement among the most frequently cited drivers ( [Burnes et al., 2021](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/18479790211016273)).

Research in _Organizational Dynamics_ argues for evidence-based approaches across the change process, including sustained communication and feedback loops ( [Hughes, 2022](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0090261622000067)).

location.heading: What supports change after the rollout phase ends?

findings.sentence: A 2025 review of 250+ studies on video-based learning found that outcomes depend heavily on design choices such as pacing, visuals, and structure ( [Navarrete et al., 2025](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40593-025-00481-x)).

location.heading: What supports change after the rollout phase ends?

findings.sentence: Research on multimedia learning shows that combining words and visuals while minimizing extraneous detail supports comprehension and transfer to real tasks ( [Mayer, 2009](https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2009-09091-000)).

meta.anchor: short, segmented content works better than one long run

location.heading: What does sustained change look like?

findings.sentence: People dip in between meetings, while multitasking, and with limited attention, so [short, segmented content works better than one long run](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/multimedia-learning/segmenting-principle/37240877DDA0362355ADB39936027982).

meta.anchor: learning takes a hit (the redundancy problem)

location.heading: What does sustained change look like?

findings.sentence: Dense on-screen text plus narration forces people to read and listen at the same time, and [learning takes a hit (the redundancy problem)](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/cambridge-handbook-of-multimedia-learning/redundancy-principle-in-multimedia-learning/448A5532008EB4B4BA17DBEB5A421920).

meta.anchor: you add extra mental work that doesn’t help them learn the task

location.heading: What does sustained change look like?

findings.sentence: When people have to split attention across dense text and visuals, [you add extra mental work that doesn’t help them learn the task](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/BF00375135).
